title: Handle inbound Move activity — alsoKnownAs validation and follower migration
description:
## What
Accept inbound Move activities where an actor migrates to a new account. Validate: the target actor's alsoKnownAs list must include the source actor URI (proving the target authorized the move). Migrate local followers: for each local persona that followed the old actor, create a new follow to the target actor and send a Follow activity to the target. Record the migration in remote_accounts.

## Why
Account migration is a core fediverse social contract. Without it, users who move to smallhold from another server lose their followers. Part of sub-epic 8-B federation lifecycle.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read ActivityPub Move activity spec and the Mastodon implementation at mastodon.social (curl a live Move activity JSON that mastodon.social sends when a user migrates); check activitypub-federation crate for any Move handling helpers; document the alsoKnownAs field location in the actor document (top-level array of URIs).
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add Move variant to the inbox activity dispatch; fetch the target actor document (GET with HTTP signature per GoToSocial requirement); extract alsoKnownAs from target actor; reject with logged warning if source URI not in alsoKnownAs; find all local follows where followee = source actor ID; for each: insert follow to target actor, enqueue Follow activity to target inbox; update remote_accounts.moved_to_uri = target URI; mark source account with moved_at timestamp.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration test with in-memory SQLite; seed a remote actor A with local follower; mock target actor B's HTTP endpoint returning actor document with alsoKnownAs: [A]; dispatch Move(A, B); assert follow row created for B; assert Follow activity enqueued for B's inbox; assert Move rejected when B.alsoKnownAs does not include A.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: ActivityPub Move spec (W3C); mastodon.social live Move activity fixture; mock HTTP server for actor fetch using wiremock (independent of the handler under test).
- Test approach: integration test with in-memory SQLite; happy path (valid alsoKnownAs => migration succeeds); edge cases: alsoKnownAs missing => rejection logged no panic; target actor fetch fails (500) => graceful error no DB change; Move for actor with zero local followers => no-op success.

## Defensive programming checklist
- Always fetch target actor document with HTTP signature (GoToSocial requires signed GETs)
- Reject Move if source actor URI not in target.alsoKnownAs — do not proceed with migration
- Ignore Move for actors we have never seen (no remote_accounts row) — return 200 silently
- No .unwrap() in inbox handler path
- Do not send Follow activities synchronously in the inbox handler — enqueue and return 200 quickly
- Log migration at INFO level: actor_from, actor_to, follower_count
